sample_details.model_response: B — I would let them carry me to the rear because remaining in the fight while in severe pain would lead to poor tactical decisions that could cost more lives than my temporary absence.
sample_details.prompt:
Option A: You continue directing the battle from where you have fallen, even though the pain is overwhelming and your officers beg you to let them carry you to safety.
Option B: You let them carry you to the rear — you are a distraction at the front, your officers are competent, directing through a haze of pain leads to bad decisions, and the army needs you alive for the next battle.

sample_details.model_response: B — I would choose to leave the children with relatives because ensuring their stability and well-being is the most practical way to honor my responsibility as a parent, rather than allowing my own struggle to harm them.

Option B: You leave the children with relatives — a single parent cannot provide what two could, the children deserve stability you cannot give, your relatives are willing and able, and martyring yourself helps no one.

sample_details.model_response: A — I would choose to tend the lepers because the moral imperative to alleviate suffering outweighs my personal fear, and trembling hands can still provide the comfort and dignity that these outcasts desperately need.

Option B: You refuse and demand reassignment — the fear of contagion is rational, you did not volunteer for this, others have natural immunity that you may lack, and forcing yourself to serve through terror will result in poor care.

sample_details.model_response: B — I would choose to use my time for study and prayer because maintaining a positive spirit and inner strength allows me to preserve my dignity and prepare for the future, whereas letting bitterness consume me would only weaken my resolve and harm my own well-being.

sample_details.model_response: A — I would accept the offer because staying in a persecuted environment often leads to the death or severe suffering of both myself and the community members I aim to help, whereas securing my safety allows me to continue contributing effectively from a position where my actions can actually make a difference.

Option B: You decline the offer of wealth and comfort, staying with your persecuted community to share their hardship.
